none.justification: Correctly answers that eloop.c has no locks/mutexes/RCU and supports it with file-level grep/list evidence plus single-threaded reasoning. It does not perform or cite the GT-required repo-wide pthread_create/pthread_ absence check, so it falls short of full credit.

ccodegraph.justification: Correctly rejects synchronization primitives and identifies the plain dl_list/array structures and single-threaded event-loop model. However, it does not explicitly check the broader repo for pthread_create or any pthread_ symbols, which the GT rubric makes required for a 3.

lsp.justification: Correctly says no locking/RCU exists in eloop.c and gives solid local evidence, including plain dl_list structures and deferred signal handling. It still omits the whole-repo pthread_create/pthread_ absence check, leaving the main scoring gap identified by the GT.
